children.heading: Establishment
children.label: a Establishment
children.text: In order to conserve, protect, and restore nationally significant landscapes that have outstanding cultural, ecological, and scientific values for the benefit of current and future generations, there is established in the Bureau of Land Management the National Landscape Conservation System.

children.label: A
children.text: a national monument;

children.label: B
children.text: a national conservation area;

children.label: C
children.text: a wilderness study area;

children.label: D
children.text: a national scenic trail or national historic trail designated as a component of the National Trails System;

children.label: E
children.text: a component of the National Wild and Scenic Rivers System; or

children.label: F
children.text: a component of the National Wilderness Preservation System.

children.label: A
children.text: the Steens Mountain Cooperative Management and Protection Area;

children.label: B
children.text: the Headwaters Forest Reserve;

children.label: C
children.text: the Yaquina Head Outstanding Natural Area;

children.label: D
children.text: public land within the California Desert Conservation Area administered by the Bureau of Land Management for conservation purposes; and

children.label: E
children.text: any additional area designated by Congress for inclusion in the system.
in accordance with any applicable law (including regulations) relating to any component of the system included under subsection (b); and
in a manner that protects the values for which the components of the system were designated.

children.label: A
children.text: the Alaska National Interest Lands Conservation Act ( et seq.);16 U.S.C. 3101

children.label: B
children.text: the Wilderness Act ( et seq.);16 U.S.C. 1131

children.label: C
children.text: the Wild and Scenic Rivers Act ( et seq.);16 U.S.C. 1271

children.label: D
children.text: the National Trails System Act ( et seq.); and16 U.S.C. 1241

children.label: E
children.text: the Federal Land Policy and Management Act of 1976 ( et seq.).43 U.S.C. 1701

children.heading: Fish and wildlife
children.label: 2 Fish and wildlife
children.text: Nothing in this chapter shall be construed as affecting the authority, jurisdiction, or responsibility of the several States to manage, control, or regulate fish and resident wildlife under State law or regulations, including the regulation of hunting, fishing, trapping and recreational shooting on public land managed by the Bureau of Land Management. Nothing in this chapter shall be construed as limiting access for hunting, fishing, trapping, or recreational shooting.
